body:
@jagoosw this PR refactors your original PR "fix bam". It addresses a few things:

1. In the original implementation of BoundaryAdjacentMean, the flux average is taken over the whole domain, but only the boundary value is used. This PR makes it so that we perform the average over only the slice adjacent to the boundary, using `view`.
2. I precompute and store the area for the flux.
3. I removed the design wherein `BoundaryAdjacentMean` acts like a callable object. Instead, I extend `getbc` to extra the flux value. Also, I noticed that there is no need to store the flux within a Ref. It is fine to store the flux in a 1-element array, which is where the reduction ends up anyways.

I also changed the name to `AverageBoundaryFlux` to emphasize the fact that this is a flux calculation.

I'd also like to mention that on the original PR, I suggested using `Average`. But in the course of this work I realize this is not possible because boundary conditions must be available _before_ a field is constructed. Because of this chicken-egg issue, it is not possible to produce a boundary condition that depends on the average of a Field without a bit of contortion (which goes well beyond merely defining `regularize_field_boundary_conditions` --- it requires intercepting the `Field` constructor itself). For now, I decided it would be best to avoid extending / changing the `Field` constructor.

If you agree with these changes, I will merge this into your PR and continue there. Let me know!
title: Refactor BoundaryAdjacentMean to AverageBoundaryFlux

body:
This PR implements a feature wherein FieldTimeSeries will automatically combine distributed data if it finds files called `filename_rank0`, `filename_rank1`, etc.

A similar output combining was implemented by @taimoorsohail here: https://github.com/taimoorsohail/ocean-ensembles/blob/ts/parallel-run/analysis/combine2d_distributed.jl

Part of this change also effectively requires `FieldTimeSeries` to work when only the prefix of a filename is provided, without the extension `.jld2`. So that is done as well.

Non-combination can still be achieved by passing the specific filename, eg `FieldTimeSeries(filename_rank0, varname)` for the data on rank0.

Closes #2351
title: Combine distributed data automagically in FieldTimeSeries
